seoTitle: Hill End vs Hill Top — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hill End and Hill Top. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hill End vs Hill Top: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hill End and Hill Top.
heading: Hill End vs Hill Top — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hill End, New South Wales and Hill Top, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hill Top has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Hill End (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hill End or Hill Top safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hill End has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Hill Top scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hill End scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Hill Top scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hill End or Hill Top?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hill Top has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
